A live activity log on the dashboard, and clearer wording for how scheduled backups and updates run.

## Added

- **A live activity log on the dashboard.** A new panel at the top of the dashboard shows — like a `docker logs` view — what BombVault is doing right now and what it did recently. A running backup or restore streams a real percentage; off-site uploads, retention pruning and integrity checks show a "working" indicator (restic gives no percentage for those, so a fake one would be dishonest); finished operations scroll into a reverse-chronological log you can scroll back through to see what happened when; and when nothing is running, the tail shows the next scheduled runs with a countdown. A filter (by domain, by type, or free text) narrows the log like `grep`. Retention prune and integrity verify now appear in the run history for the first time, so they are no longer invisible.

## Changed

- **Clearer wording for "update after backup" and the container schedule (#95).** The update-after-backup option now says plainly that it recreates a container right after its backup — at the backup's (sequential) time, not a fixed clock time — and points to ShipLog for updates on a set, version-gated schedule. The container schedule now notes that included containers back up one after another from the scheduled time, because they share one repository and run in sequence (this is inherent, not a bug — parallelizing them is the lock contention fixed in v6.5.0).
